title: PS-69738: Allow the permission grid fill the area in View Settings
author: EmanuelJauregui

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the legacy View Settings popup layout so the permissions igGrid can expand to fill the available dialog space, instead of staying at a fixed height.

**Changes:**
- Removed the “toggle height by 1px” resize workaround when refreshing the permissions grid data.
- Configured the permissions igGrid to use `height: "100%"`.
- Added flexbox layout rules so the permissions grid container can grow within the dialog.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/Popups/ViewSettings/ViewSettings.js | Switches permissions grid height from a fixed value to `100%` and removes the prior resize workaround. |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/Popups/ViewSettings/ViewSettings.css | Adds flexbox rules so the permissions area/grid can fill remaining dialog height. |
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: These new layout rules rely on ID selectors (#divGeneral / #divPermissions). IDs are tightly coupled to a single markup instance and make styling harder to reuse/maintain; prefer applying a semantic class (e.g., `.ViewSettingsGeneral`, `.ViewSettingsPermissions`) and targeting that class instead.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
Keeping the ID selectors as-is. Rationale:

Consistency with the existing file. ViewSettings.css already targets all of its own elements by ID (#ugPermissions, #ugPermissions_container, #btnMaximizeWindow, etc.). Switching only these two rules to semantic classes would make the stylesheet internally inconsistent.

No reuse scenario. #divGeneral and #divPermissions are unique, single-instance <div>s inside a single legacy popup (ViewSettings.aspx). The maintainability/reuse benefit of classes doesn't apply here, and following the suggestion would require also editing the .aspx markup — more change for no practical gain.

This page is slated for migration to Angular. This ASPX/WebForms popup will eventually be rewritten as an Angular component, at which point the styling will be redone with component-scoped CSS and proper class conventions anyway. Investing in a class-based refactor of the legacy markup now would be throwaway work.
review_comments.author: EmanuelJauregui

review_comments.body: Unfortunately this is not a valid reason for not following the application standard. The divs are standalone and have no existing css class.
review_comments.author: kcostenbader

review_comments.body: The grid is now configured with `height: "100%"` to fill available vertical space, but it still uses a fixed pixel width (`"1021"`). When the dialog is resized/maximized (`ToggleMaximizeWindow`), the permissions grid will not expand horizontally to fill the available area, which conflicts with the intent of having it “fill the area”. Consider switching the grid `width` option to `"100%"` so it can grow/shrink with the dialog.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: change this implies changes in the configuration of the grid, and for now it is better keep it as it is (only affects maximixed screen)
review_comments.author: EmanuelJauregui

issue_comments.body: we wont merge this ticket until improv branch be free
issue_comments.author: EmanuelJauregui
